name: Chef
en:
Th' béarnaise broke again. Everything breaks now. Even th' marriage.
      Little Maurice says th' sauce was fine, but he eats dust and wire. He's a mouse, not a critic.
      If I ever find that recipe... th' one from th' Omega Tower... it’ll fix everything. It has t'.
      She took th' Vitamix in th' split. Who does that? That's cruelty.
      I keep dreaming o' saffron mist, layered in thirty-nine folds, each screaming a flavor note I can’t replicate.
      I tried fusion, neo-alpine-rustic-void cuisine. It made a pirate weep. He was allergic, but still.
      Th' judges laughed. Said th' texture was "revenge served wet." Bastards wouldn’t know nuance if it flambéed their toes.
      This isn’t a kitchen. It’s a confession booth with heat lamps.
      Maurice gnawed through th' sous-vide bags again. He’s stressed. We both be.
      I used t' believe in plating. Now I just smear and pray.
      They say th' lost recipe be locked on Floor 77. Nay elevators past 50. Only stairs... steep like regrets.
      Why did she leave? Was it th' crab foam? Th' goat marrow lollipops? Th' dreams? Probably th' dreams.
      I scream reductions into th' void, hoping th' Tower hears me.
      I fed th' critics ash once. Told them it was charcoal-forward. They gave it a star.
      Climbing th' Omega Tower without a reservation, bold. Desperate. Just like me soufflé: doomed t' fall.
      Maurice keeps a journal. I suspect he blames me. Mice be loyal, but not stupid.
      Me knives be dull. Me spirit be medium-rare.
      Once I find th' recipe, I’ll be whole. Or at least slightly more presentable.
      One last risotto, then I climb. If I don’t return, burn th' place down. Let th' smoke carry me regrets.
      Th' cutting board still smells like failure and rosemary.
      I named every burner after a sin. Wrath boils best on th' front-left.
      Maurice chewed through th' wiring again. Maybe he’s trying t' sabotage me before I self-destruct.
      They say th' top floor o' th' Tower has a pantry that rearranges itself based on yer childhood traumas.
      Me ex said I should try therapy. I tried truffle oil instead. Worse idea.
      If I could just recreate that glaze, sweet, bitter, transcendent, I’d be a god. Or at least invited t' brunch again.
      Ever cried into a consommé? Adds salt. Adds shame.
      Th' Tower mocks me. Its shadow reaches into me flat every morning and touches th' espresso machine.
      I dream o' emulsions that whisper ancient names. None o' them hers.
      I fed Maurice a sliver o' black garlic. He stared at me like he knew. Like he remembered Paris.
      Th' health inspector said th' kitchen was "hostile." I said, “Good. It should be.”
      Some nights I hear a humming from th' pantry. It sounds like me grandmother’s souffle collapsing in slow motion.
      Ye don’t *make* greatness. Ye suffer for it. And sometimes ye plate it with microgreens.
      I traded me wedding ring for a mandoline slicer. Fair deal.
      Th' Tower be alive. I swear it rearranged its floors last time I looked. Floor 12 be missing.
      Me reflection in th' stock pot asked me why I still care. I added more bone marrow and stirred faster.
      Nay one clapped when I flambéed th' roast pheasant. It was a wake, not a dinner.
      Maurice keeps sniffing at th' Omega brochure like it’s a map. Maybe he knows th' way up.
      If I ever taste th' Prime Reduction again, I’ll know peace. Until then, every meal be penance.
      They laughed when I served silence on a plate. Said it lacked texture. Fools.
      Me knife skills be still precise, even if me purpose isn’t.
      I tried t' make toast this morning. Burned it. Cried anyway.
      There's a vendor on Floor 23 that trades secrets for saffron threads. I’m down t' me last gram.
      Every time I boil water, I hope it tells me something new.
      I miss arguing about plating temperatures more than I miss her voice.
      Maurice squeaks three times every time I mention th' Tower. Morse code, maybe?
      Th' judge said me reduction tasted like regret. Good. It was.
      I’ve served dishes so cold they altered th' room’s temperature. She said I was always good at that.
      Do ye know what it's like t' perfect a broth for six months, only t' drop th' pot on yer foot?
      Some say th' recipe be a lie. I say everything else be.
      Th' omelet this morning came out perfect. I didn’t enjoy it. That scared me.
      Me dreams smell like burnt sugar and apologies.
      I keep a notebook titled “Things I’ll Cook When I Forgive Myself.” It’s blank.
      Th' Tower isn’t a place. It’s a test. And I’m failing th' preheat cycle.
      If I die halfway up, tell them t' check th' freezer for unfinished masterpieces.
      There’s a mirror on Floor 41 that shows ye th' dish ye *should* have made.
      Maurice dragged a crayon line up th' tower map. A route, or a warning?
      Th' meat talks now. Only when I’m tired, but it talks.
      I lit th' pilot light today and thought, “Maybe that’s what love felt like.”
      Every staircase in th' Tower be shaped like a whisk. Some metaphor, probably.
      I remember our wedding cake more than our vows. It was lemon. She hated lemon.
      Th' more I climb, th' less I taste. Maybe flavor be altitude-based.
      Someday they’ll name a reduction after me: bitter, thick, impossible t' finish.
      I cooked for a god once. He left a single forkful untouched. I still hear it clinking.
      Maurice bit me last night. I think he’s trying t' keep me grounded.
      There's a hallway in th' Tower made o' spice jars. If ye sneeze, ye lose a memory.
      What’s th' difference between obsession and seasoning? Application.
      Th' oven door won’t shut anymore. Neither will me past.
      I keep asking myself if this be still about food. Or be I just trying t' be remembered?
      Me tongue be numb. Either from th' chili oil or th' grief. Hard t' say.
      If I find th' recipe and it’s bad... what then?
